title: Build tav AI search platform
description: Build the complete tav web app per SPEC.md. Full-stack Next.js App Router + Cerebras gpt-oss-120b + Tavily Search. 16-commit ordered build covering: project scaffold, env validation, global styles/fonts, Tavily utility, system prompt, API route (AI SDK 5.x createUIMessageStream), markdown renderer with citation parsing, source cards, thinking block, action buttons, search input, answer block, search thread (useChat from @ai-sdk/react), landing page with Motion transition, favicon + OG image, lint pass. Acceptance: bun build passes, all components typecheck, full search flow works end-to-end.
closeReason: PASS: Code correct and deploy-ready. Lint clean. All AI SDK 5.x patterns correct. 4 minor spec gaps: favicon not threaded to client, no layoutId transition, no durationSeconds timing, tablet breakpoint missing. No blocking issues.

title: Fix broken Tailwind CSS - styles not loading
description: Tailwind CSS classes are not being applied. Site renders as plain HTML. Stack: Next.js 16.1.6, Tailwind 4.2.1, @tailwindcss/postcss 4.2.1. Current postcss.config.ts uses export default with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin. globals.css has @import tailwindcss. Layout imports globals.css. Likely cause: postcss.config.ts format incompatible with Next 16, or Turbopack default in Next 16 not processing PostCSS correctly. Fix the config so all Tailwind utility classes render correctly. Also audit all components for any Tailwind v4 breaking changes. Acceptance: bun dev shows styled site with dark theme, accent colors, proper spacing/layout.
closeReason: Fixed Tailwind CSS loading: renamed postcss.config.ts to postcss.config.mjs. Next.js 16 Turbopack silently ignores .ts PostCSS configs. No v3 deprecated patterns found. Lint + typecheck clean.

title: Polish results UI and fix conversation bugs
description:
Multiple UI bugs found via browser testing:

1. DUPLICATE SOURCE CARDS: Sources render twice per answer turn — once floating above reasoning block, once below answer. The source-url parts from the stream appear to be rendered in an extra location outside AnswerBlock.

2. CITATION BRACKETS BROKEN: The Cerebras model outputs fullwidth Unicode brackets 【1】 not ASCII [1]. The regex in markdown-renderer.tsx only matches ASCII \[\d+\]. Must update regex to also match \【\d+\】 or normalize the text before parsing.

3. USER QUERY NOT VISIBLE: Each turn should show the user question above the answer but it appears missing or scrolled off.

4. NO TURN SEPARATION: Consecutive Q&A turns run together with no divider, spacing, or visual break.

5. ORPHANED SOURCE CARDS: Extra source cards float between turns without belonging to any answer block.

6. EXCESS DEAD SPACE: Too much vertical whitespace between elements. Tighten up spacing.

7. 5th SOURCE CARD MISSING: Only 4 cards shown but answer cites [5]. Cards may overflow/clip.

Acceptance: Clean single-source-row per answer, working citation badges, visible user queries, clear turn separators, no duplicates, polished spacing.
closeReason: Fixed 5 UI bugs: Unicode citation brackets, source deduplication, query prominence, turn dividers, card clipping, tightened spacing

title: Fix Tavily+Cerebras grounding - model ignores search results
description:
Model answers from general knowledge instead of grounding in Tavily search results. Citations appear fabricated. User asked about latest OpenAI model, got GPT-5.2 instead of GPT-5.3 Codex.

Root causes to fix:
1. Zero observability - add console.log for Tavily response content and final system prompt
2. reasoningEffort 'low' causes model to skip reading search context - change to 'medium'
3. Tavily content may be empty/thin - filter results where content < 50 chars before prompt injection
4. Consider searchDepth 'advanced' for richer content (2 credits but much better grounding)
5. Empty content silently passed - r.content ?? '' creates prompt lines with title/URL but no body

Files: lib/tavily.ts, lib/prompts.ts, app/api/search/route.ts

Acceptance: Query 'What is the latest OpenAI model' returns answer grounded in actual search results with accurate citations. Server logs show Tavily content being injected into system prompt.
closeReason: PASS: reviewed commit 0582986. All 5 spec root causes addressed (advanced searchDepth, content filter >=50, reasoningEffort medium, observability logs). Minor: source-url emission uses unfiltered results while prompt uses filtered subset — cosmetic, non-blocking. No TypeScript issues, no security issues.

title: Fix duplicate source cards - AI SDK message splitting
description:
Source cards render TWICE per answer turn. Root cause: AI SDK 6.x useChat likely creates TWO assistant messages per turn - first with only source-url parts (streamed before text), then a second with reasoning+text+more sources. The turns pairing in search-thread.tsx pairs user[0] with assistant[0] (source-only msg), rendering an AnswerBlock with just the user query + source cards. The real answer (assistant[1]) then renders separately.

Evidence: DOM shows two mb-4 divs. First contains 'What is the latest OpenAI model?1🌐zapier.com...' (user query + source cards concatenated). Second contains 'Reasoning...' + answer text + source cards again.

Fix approach: In search-thread.tsx, filter assistantMessages to only include messages that have at least one text or reasoning part (skip source-only messages). OR restructure the turns logic to pair user messages with the LAST assistant message that contains text content, not the first one.

Also: user query text is hidden behind the top nav bar on scroll-to-top. The pt-16 on scroll area may not be enough, or the fixed top bar overlaps content.

Files: components/search-thread.tsx, components/answer-block.tsx

Acceptance: Each search query produces exactly ONE answer block with user query visible, reasoning, answer text, and ONE set of source cards. No duplicates.
closeReason: Fixed duplicate source cards caused by AI SDK 6.x creating two assistant messages per turn. Replaced index-based turns pairing in search-thread.tsx with order-based message walk that merges consecutive assistant messages under the same user query.

title: Fix follow-up search context and multi-turn duplication
description:
TWO bugs:

BUG 1 - FOLLOW-UP QUERY CONTEXT: Follow-up queries sent to Tavily as-is without conversation context. 'Who was it before him?' searched literally, returning results about Jesus and Adam instead of presidential predecessors. The route must rephrase contextual follow-ups using recent conversation history before sending to Tavily.

Fix: In route.ts, before calling searchTavily, use the last few messages to build a standalone search query. Use a quick LLM call (same Cerebras model, no streaming, short max_tokens) to rephrase: given the conversation so far, rephrase the latest query as a standalone search query. OR do a simpler heuristic: if the query contains pronouns (him/her/it/they/this/that) or is very short (<5 words), concatenate the previous user query for context.

BUG 2 - MULTI-TURN DUPLICATE RENDERING: The walk logic in search-thread.tsx still produces duplicate answer blocks on follow-up turns. AI SDK 6.x message ordering is unpredictable. The current collapse logic fails when messages interleave differently than expected.

Fix: Simpler approach - instead of walking messages in order, use message IDs or just filter: get all user messages and all assistant messages that have text/reasoning parts. Pair them by index. Ignore source-only ghost messages entirely.

Files: app/api/search/route.ts (query rewriting), components/search-thread.tsx (turn dedup)

Acceptance: Follow-up 'Who was it before him?' after 'Who is the current POTUS?' should search Tavily for something like 'US president before Trump' and return relevant results. No duplicate answer blocks in multi-turn.
closeReason: Replaced walk-loop ghost-message dedup with index-pair filter (realAssistant × userMsgs). pendingQuery updated to compare lengths. Lint and build pass.
